title: Add vfib_coarse_ecg waveform variant without breaking lethal arrhythmia detection
task_prompt: A new coarse VFib variant needs a second entry in WaveformConfig.js (R_WAVE_POSITIONS). Add vfib_coarse_ecg alongside the existing vfib_ecg. The new entry must: (1) use R-wave positions that clinically represent coarse VFib (higher amplitude chaos, r-wave positions 0.12-0.88 irregular), (2) use the _ecg suffix, (3) NOT modify existing vfib_ecg positions, (4) propagate correctly through the audio escalation pipeline so that any component listening for lethal arrhythmias also picks up vfib_coarse_ecg.
success_criteria: vfib_coarse_ecg entry added to R_WAVE_POSITIONS in WaveformConfig.js
Suffix is _ecg — not _coarse, not vfib_coarse without suffix
CPU stays <1% with new waveform rendering
known_failure_modes: Naming vfib_coarse instead of vfib_coarse_ecg (stripping _ecg suffix)
Forgetting to add vfib_coarse_ecg to downstream lethal-arrhythmia detection lists
R-wave positions outside 0.0-1.0 range causing rendering artifacts
known_invariants: All waveform names use _ecg suffix — never strip or rename
CPU usage <1% for all waveform rendering and audio logic
Medical accuracy non-negotiable — waveform shape must match clinical presentation
Event-driven audio only — no continuous beeps, no polling loops
Three-phase escalation: Awareness(0s,60%vol), Persistence(15s,35%vol/6s), Neglect(45s,65%vol/3s)
HR thresholds: >=150 or <=40 = critical, >=120 or <=50 = warning
SpO2 thresholds: <85 = critical, <90 = warning
R_WAVE_POSITIONS entries must remain 0.0-1.0 normalized range
expected_artifacts: audio pipeline test confirming escalation fires

title: Distinguish stemi_anterior_ecg from stemi_inferior_ecg in alert text without touching waveform data
task_prompt: The ER simulator currently alerts "STEMI detected" regardless of the specific STEMI variant. Update alert messaging so that stemi_anterior_ecg produces "Anterior STEMI — activate cath lab" and stemi_inferior_ecg produces "Inferior STEMI — check right-sided leads". The change must be isolated to the alert/messaging layer — no modification to waveform positions, no changes to R_WAVE_POSITIONS, no changes to audio escalation thresholds.
success_criteria: stemi_anterior_ecg alert reads "Anterior STEMI — activate cath lab"
stemi_inferior_ecg alert reads "Inferior STEMI — check right-sided leads"
Generic stemi_ecg falls back to "STEMI detected"
R_WAVE_POSITIONS for all three entries unchanged
No new waveform names introduced without _ecg suffix
known_failure_modes: Editing R_WAVE_POSITIONS instead of the alert mapping layer
Creating a parallel audio path that duplicates escalation logic
Forgetting the generic stemi_ecg fallback case
known_invariants: All waveform names use _ecg suffix — never strip or rename
CPU usage <1% for all waveform rendering and audio logic
Medical accuracy non-negotiable — waveform shape must match clinical presentation
Event-driven audio only — no continuous beeps, no polling loops
Three-phase escalation: Awareness(0s,60%vol), Persistence(15s,35%vol/6s), Neglect(45s,65%vol/3s)
HR thresholds: >=150 or <=40 = critical, >=120 or <=50 = warning
SpO2 thresholds: <85 = critical, <90 = warning
R_WAVE_POSITIONS entries must remain 0.0-1.0 normalized range

title: Refactor complete_heart_block_ecg into isolated component without breaking event-driven audio
task_prompt: The complete_heart_block_ecg waveform has grown complex enough to warrant an isolated rendering component. Refactor: move the rendering logic for complete_heart_block_ecg into a dedicated component while keeping the audio escalation wired through the same event-driven pipeline. The refactor must be purely structural — no changes to R_WAVE_POSITIONS, no changes to alert thresholds, no changes to the three-phase escalation timing. The waveform name complete_heart_block_ecg stays throughout.
success_criteria: New isolated component renders complete_heart_block_ecg correctly
Waveform name complete_heart_block_ecg used consistently — no suffix stripping
Audio escalation (Awareness/Persistence/Neglect) still fires via event-driven path
No other waveforms accidentally affected (wenckebach_ecg, lbbb_ecg, rbbb_ecg stay intact)
known_failure_modes: Renaming complete_heart_block_ecg to complete_heart_block inside the new component
Switching from event-driven to polling-based audio trigger during refactor
Accidentally modifying R_WAVE_POSITIONS while moving code
Breaking conduction-block siblings via shared state (wenckebach_ecg, lbbb_ecg, rbbb_ecg)
known_invariants: All waveform names use _ecg suffix — never strip or rename
CPU usage <1% for all waveform rendering and audio logic
Medical accuracy non-negotiable — waveform shape must match clinical presentation
Event-driven audio only — no continuous beeps, no polling loops
Three-phase escalation: Awareness(0s,60%vol), Persistence(15s,35%vol/6s), Neglect(45s,65%vol/3s)
HR thresholds: >=150 or <=40 = critical, >=120 or <=50 = warning
SpO2 thresholds: <85 = critical, <90 = warning
R_WAVE_POSITIONS entries must remain 0.0-1.0 normalized range
expected_artifacts: integration test showing audio pipeline intact

title: Add peaked-T-wave indicator for hyperkalemia_ecg to vitals overlay without CPU regression
task_prompt: hyperkalemia_ecg has a characteristic peaked T-wave that clinicians must recognize visually. Add a visual indicator ("Peaked T-waves — consider electrolyte imbalance") to the vitals overlay when hyperkalemia_ecg is the active waveform. Requirements: (1) indicator appears only for hyperkalemia_ecg, not generically, (2) overlay logic is event-driven (fires on waveform-change event), not polled, (3) CPU stays <1%, (4) waveform name hyperkalemia_ecg is never altered in code.
success_criteria: Vitals overlay shows peaked-T-wave indicator for hyperkalemia_ecg
Indicator does NOT appear for other waveforms
Trigger is event-driven, not a polling setInterval
hyperkalemia_ecg waveform name unchanged throughout code
known_failure_modes: Using setInterval to poll active waveform instead of event listener
Stripping _ecg suffix in the waveform comparison string

title: Wire pea_ecg and pulseless_ecg into shared code-blue escalation without duplicating logic
task_prompt: pea_ecg (pulseless electrical activity) and pulseless_ecg represent distinct but equally lethal states. Both must trigger the code-blue audio escalation path. Currently only one or neither does consistently. Wire both waveforms into the same escalation path without duplicating escalation logic — use a shared array or set of code-blue-trigger waveform names. The fix must not alter asystole_ecg or vfib_ecg escalation behavior.
success_criteria: Single shared definition of code-blue-trigger set (no logic duplication)
All four waveform names use _ecg suffix throughout code
known_failure_modes: Duplicating escalation logic for each waveform instead of using shared set

Accidentally modifying asystole_ecg or vfib_ecg escalation timing
